seo.title: AI Agent Hallucination Detection in Google ADK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent hallucination detection in Google ADK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Google ADK, AI agent hallucination manifests as agents generating fabricated facts, inconsistent tool responses, or inventing session state during multi-agent workflows powered by Gemini models. This is exacerbated by delegation loops where agents reference non-existent prior outputs and session corruption in long conversations leading to fabricated context. Tool mismatches across agents further propagate hallucinations as one agent's parsed output becomes another's invented input.

howItManifests.symptom: Agent B claims Agent A approved a $50K budget increase that never occurred
howItManifests.impact: Cascading false decisions across workflow, financial authorization errors

howItManifests.scenario: Gemini model switching causing output inconsistency
howItManifests.symptom: Same query returns 'Paris population: 2.1M' then '12.5M' across model switches
howItManifests.impact: Downstream agents use conflicting data, analytics completely wrong

howItManifests.scenario: Session state corruption in long conversations
howItManifests.symptom: Agent references 'Q3 earnings call' that happened in hallucinated session history
howItManifests.impact: Strategic decisions based on fabricated corporate events

howItManifests.scenario: Tool response format mismatches across agents
howItManifests.symptom: Agent A returns JSON {'price': 299}, Agent B hallucinates as {'price_usd': 29900}
howItManifests.impact: Pricing errors compound through e-commerce workflow

detection.name: Cross-Agent Fact Consistency Check
detection.description: Detects when delegated agents reference facts not present in parent agent outputs using ADK session tracing

detection.name: Gemini Output Variance Monitor
detection.description: Compares identical prompts across Gemini model switches in ADK workflows

detection.name: Session State Reality Check
detection.description: Validates agent references against actual ADK session history

fixes.name: Fact Grounding Middleware
fixes.description: Intercepts ADK agent outputs and validates claims against source documents before delegation

fixes.name: Model Consistency Lock
fixes.description: Forces ADK workflow to single Gemini model variant, caches verified responses

fixes.name: Session State Validator
fixes.description: ADK callback that rewrites outputs referencing invalid session state

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Hallucination Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a before_agent_callback in Google ADK workflows, automatically detecting all four hallucination patterns across multi-agent delegation chains. It maintains a shadow ground-truth database of verified facts from session history and external sources, scoring agent outputs in real-time against this baseline. When hallucinations exceed configurable thresholds, Vex triggers automatic remediation using the fixes above or human-in-loop escalation.

The platform provides ADK-specific observability dashboards showing hallucination hotspots by agent, model variant, and delegation path. Vex's adaptive monitoring learns your workflow's normal fact patterns, reducing false positives over time while catching novel hallucinations. Integration takes <5 minutes and works with existing ADK session management.

Vex also auto-generates remediation code for your specific hallucination patterns, deploying fact-grounding middleware directly into your ADK agents without code changes.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import VexMonitor
from google_adk import Workflow

# Install: pip install google-adk vex-sdk

vex = VexMonitor(
    detectors=[
        'cross_agent_consistency',
        'gemini_variance',
        'session_state_corruption',
        'tool_format_mismatch'
    ],
    auto_remediate=True,
    adk_integration=True
)

class MonitoredWorkflow(Workflow):
    def before_agent_callback(self, ctx):
        # Vex auto-instrumentation
        return vex.monitor_agent(ctx)
    
# Usage - Vex handles everything automatically
workflow = MonitoredWorkflow(agents=[agent1, agent2, agent3])
result = workflow.run(task)

# View hallucination dashboard
vex.dashboard()

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add one line of Vex integration to your Google ADK workflow and eliminate agent hallucinations forever.
